Package algatorgraph

Class DGraph<V,​W>

  • java.lang.Object
    • algatorgraph.DGraph<V,​W>
  • Type Parameters:
    V - the type of vertices
    W - the type of weights on edges
    All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable, org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​DEdge<V,​W>>
    public class DGraph<V,​W>
extends java.lang.Object
implements org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​DEdge<V,​W>>, java.io.Serializable
    Represents a directed graph using an adjacency list representation. The graph supports generic vertex and edge types, with edges having a generic weight type. The adjacency lists are stored in a HashMaps.

    Example usage:

    
 // Create a graph with String vertices and Integer weights
 DGraph<String, Integer> cityGraph = new DGraph<>();
 
 // Add vertices (cities)
 cityGraph.addVertex("New York");
 cityGraph.addVertex("Boston");
 
 // Add a weighted edge (distance between cities)
 cityGraph.addEdge("New York", "Boston", new DEdge<>("New York", "Boston", 215));
 
 // Get all edges
 Set<DEdge<String, Integer>> allRoutes = cityGraph.edgeSet();
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from interface org.jgrapht.Graph

        DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHT

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      DGraph()
      Constructor creates a new empty HashMap()

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      DEdge<V,​W> addEdge​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Adds an edge connecting the specified source and target vertices, creating and returning a new edge.
      boolean addEdge​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex, DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Adds the specified edge to the graph, connecting the specified source and target vertices.
      V addVertex()  
      boolean addVertex​(V vertex)
      Adds the specified vertex to the graph.
      boolean containsEdge​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Checks if the graph contains the specified edge.
      boolean containsEdge​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Checks if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices.
      boolean containsVertex​(V vertex)
      Checks if the graph contains the specified vertex.
      int degreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the degree of the specified vertex, which is the total number of edges incident to the vertex.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> edgeSet()
      Returns the set of all edges in the graph.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> edgesOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the set of edges connected to the specified vertex.
      boolean equals​(java.lang.Object obj)
      Compares this graph to the specified object for equality.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> getAllEdges​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Returns all edges connecting the specified source and target vertices.
      DEdge<V,​W> getEdge​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Returns the edge connecting the specified source and target vertices.
      V getEdgeSource​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the source vertex of the specified edge.
      java.util.function.Supplier<DEdge<V,​W>> getEdgeSupplier()
      Not implemented.
      V getEdgeTarget​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the target vertex of the specified edge.
      double getEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Returns the weight of the specified edge.
      org.jgrapht.GraphType getType()
      Returns the type of this graph.
      java.util.function.Supplier<V> getVertexSupplier()
      Not implemented.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> incomingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the set of edges incoming to the specified vertex.
      int inDegreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the in-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of incoming edges to the vertex.
      int outDegreeOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the out-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of outgoing edges from the vertex.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> outgoingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
      Returns the set of edges outgoing from the specified vertex.
      boolean removeAllEdges​(java.util.Collection<? extends DEdge<V,​W>> edges)
      Removes all specified edges from the graph.
      java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> removeAllEdges​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Removes all edges connecting the specified source and target vertices.
      boolean removeAllVertices​(java.util.Collection<? extends V> vertices)
      Removes all specified vertices and their incident edges from the graph.
      boolean removeEdge​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
      Removes the specified edge from the graph.
      DEdge<V,​W> removeEdge​(V sourceVertex, V targetVertex)
      Removes the edge connecting the specified source and target vertices.
      boolean removeVertex​(V vertex)
      Removes the specified vertex and all its edges from the graph.
      void setEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge, double weight)
      Sets the weight of the specified edge to the given double value.
      void setEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge, W weight)
      Sets edge weight
      java.lang.String toString()
      Returns a string representation of the graph.
      java.util.Set<V> vertexSet()
      Returns the set of all vertices in the graph.

    • Constructor Detail

      • DGraph

        public DGraph()
        Constructor creates a new empty HashMap()

    • Method Detail

      • edgeSet

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> edgeSet()
        Returns the set of all edges in the graph.
        Specified by:
        edgeSet in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        the set of all edges in the graph Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges.

      • edgesOf

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> edgesOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the set of edges connected to the specified vertex.
        Specified by:
        edgesOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose edges are to be returned
        Returns:
        the set of edges connected to the specified vertex Time complexity: O(1) for lookup, O(deg(v)) for copying, where deg(v) is the degree of the vertex

      • removeEdge

        public boolean removeEdge​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Removes the specified edge from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge to be removed
        Returns:
        true if the edge was successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1) average case for HashSet removal

      • removeVertex

        public boolean removeVertex​(V vertex)
        Removes the specified vertex and all its edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeVertex in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex to be removed
        Returns:
        true if the vertex was successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges. Explanation: This method removes the vertex and iterates through all edges to remove edges pointing to the vertex.

      • vertexSet

        public java.util.Set<V> vertexSet()
        Returns the set of all vertices in the graph.
        Specified by:
        vertexSet in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        the set of all vertices in the graph Time complexity: O(V), where V is the number of vertices. Explanation: This method creates a set of keys from the adjacency list map.

      • getEdge

        public DEdge<V,​W> getEdge​(V sourceVertex,
                                V targetVertex)
        Returns the edge connecting the specified source and target vertices.
        Specified by:
        getEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        the edge connecting the specified vertices, or null if no such edge exists Time complexity: O(deg(sourceVertex)), where deg is the out-degree of the source vertex Explanation: This method searches through the list of edges for the source vertex.

      • getAllEdges

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> getAllEdges​(V sourceVertex,
                                                   V targetVertex)
        Returns all edges connecting the specified source and target vertices.
        Specified by:
        getAllEdges in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        a set of all edges connecting the specified vertices Time complexity: O(deg(sourceVertex)), where deg is the out-degree of the source vertex. Explanation: This method filters the list of edges for the source vertex to find all edges connecting to the target vertex.

      • getEdgeSupplier

        public java.util.function.Supplier<DEdge<V,​W>> getEdgeSupplier()
        Not implemented.
        Specified by:
        getEdgeSupplier in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        a supplier for creating new edges Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method returns a supplier that creates a new edge with null values.

      • getVertexSupplier

        public java.util.function.Supplier<V> getVertexSupplier()
        Not implemented.
        Specified by:
        getVertexSupplier in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        a supplier for creating new vertices Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method throws an UnsupportedOperationException.

      • addEdge

        public DEdge<V,​W> addEdge​(V sourceVertex,
                                V targetVertex)
        Adds an edge connecting the specified source and target vertices, creating and returning a new edge.
        Specified by:
        addEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        the newly created edge, or null if the edge could not be added Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method creates a new edge and adds it to the adjacency list for the source vertex.

      • addEdge

        public boolean addEdge​(V sourceVertex,
                       V targetVertex,
                       DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Adds the specified edge to the graph, connecting the specified source and target vertices.
        Specified by:
        addEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        edge - the edge to be added
        Returns:
        true if the edge was successfully added, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1).

        public V addVertex()
        Specified by:

        Returns:
        the newly added vertex

      • addVertex

        public boolean addVertex​(V vertex)
        Adds the specified vertex to the graph.
        Specified by:
        addVertex in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex to be added
        Returns:
        true if the vertex was successfully added, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method adds a new vertex to the adjacency list.

      • containsEdge

        public boolean containsEdge​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Checks if the graph contains the specified edge.
        Specified by:
        containsEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge to check for
        Returns:
        true if the graph contains the specified edge, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1) average case for HashSet lookup

      • containsEdge

        public boolean containsEdge​(V sourceVertex,
                            V targetVertex)
        Checks if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices.
        Specified by:
        containsEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        true if the graph contains an edge connecting the specified vertices, false otherwise Time complexity: O(deg(sourceVertex)), where deg is the out-degree of the source vertex

      • containsVertex

        public boolean containsVertex​(V vertex)
        Checks if the graph contains the specified vertex.
        Specified by:
        containsVertex in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex to check for
        Returns:
        true if the graph contains the specified vertex, false otherwise Time complexity: O(1).

      • degreeOf

        public int degreeOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the degree of the specified vertex, which is the total number of edges incident to the vertex.
        Specified by:
        degreeOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose degree is to be calculated
        Returns:
        the degree of the specified vertex Time complexity: O(E) + O(1) = O(E). Explanation: This method calls and sums the numbers of incoming and outgoing edges.

      • getEdgeSource

        public V getEdgeSource​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Returns the source vertex of the specified edge.
        Specified by:
        getEdgeSource in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge whose source vertex is to be returned
        Returns:
        the source vertex of the specified edge Time complexity: O(1).

      • getEdgeTarget

        public V getEdgeTarget​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Returns the target vertex of the specified edge.
        Specified by:
        getEdgeTarget in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge whose target vertex is to be returned
        Returns:
        the target vertex of the specified edge Time complexity: O(1).

      • getEdgeWeight

        public double getEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge)
        Returns the weight of the specified edge. If weight is null we return 0 and 1 if the weight cannot be cast to a Number type.
        Specified by:
        getEdgeWeight in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge whose weight is to be returned
        Returns:
        the weight of the specified edge Time complexity: O(1).

      • getType

        public org.jgrapht.GraphType getType()
        Returns the type of this graph. This implementation returns null.
        Specified by:
        getType in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Returns:
        the type of this graph Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method returns null as a placeholder for the graph type.

      • inDegreeOf

        public int inDegreeOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the in-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of incoming edges to the vertex.
        Specified by:
        inDegreeOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose in-degree is to be calculated
        Returns:
        the in-degree of the specified vertex Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges. Explanation: This method iterates through all edges to count the ones targeting the vertex.

      • incomingEdgesOf

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> incomingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the set of edges incoming to the specified vertex.
        Specified by:
        incomingEdgesOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose incoming edges are to be returned
        Returns:
        the set of edges incoming to the specified vertex Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges. Explanation: This method iterates through all edges to find those targeting the vertex.

      • outDegreeOf

        public int outDegreeOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the out-degree of the specified vertex, which is the number of outgoing edges from the vertex.
        Specified by:
        outDegreeOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose out-degree is to be calculated
        Returns:
        the out-degree of the specified vertex Time complexity: O(1). Explanation: This method returns the size of the adjacency list for the vertex.

      • outgoingEdgesOf

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> outgoingEdgesOf​(V vertex)
        Returns the set of edges outgoing from the specified vertex.
        Specified by:
        outgoingEdgesOf in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertex - the vertex whose outgoing edges are to be returned
        Returns:
        the set of edges outgoing from the specified vertex Time complexity: O(1) for lookup, O(deg(v)) for copying, where deg(v) is the out-degree of the vertex Explanation: This method creates a set of edges from the adjacency list of the vertex.

      • removeAllEdges

        public boolean removeAllEdges​(java.util.Collection<? extends DEdge<V,​W>> edges)
        Removes all specified edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeAllEdges in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edges - the edges to be removed
        Returns:
        true if any edges were successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(E), where E is the number of edges. Explanation: This method iterates through all adjacency lists to remove the specified edges.

      • removeAllEdges

        public java.util.Set<DEdge<V,​W>> removeAllEdges​(V sourceVertex,
                                                      V targetVertex)
        Removes all edges connecting the specified source and target vertices.
        Specified by:
        removeAllEdges in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        the set of removed edges Time complexity: O(deg(sourceVertex)), where deg(sourceVertex) is the out-degree of the source vertex. Explanation: This method filters the edges of the source vertex to remove edges connecting to the target vertex.

      • removeAllVertices

        public boolean removeAllVertices​(java.util.Collection<? extends V> vertices)
        Removes all specified vertices and their incident edges from the graph.
        Specified by:
        removeAllVertices in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        vertices - the vertices to be removed
        Returns:
        true if any vertices were successfully removed, false otherwise Time complexity: O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges. Explanation: This method removes each specified vertex and all edges connected to it.

      • removeEdge

        public DEdge<V,​W> removeEdge​(V sourceVertex,
                                   V targetVertex)
        Removes the edge connecting the specified source and target vertices.
        Specified by:
        removeEdge in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        sourceVertex - the source vertex
        targetVertex - the target vertex
        Returns:
        the removed edge, or null if no such edge existed Time complexity: O(deg(sourceVertex)), where deg is the out-degree of the source vertex. Explanation: This method searches through the list of edges for the source vertex to find and remove the edge to the target vertex.

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Returns a string representation of the graph.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a string representation of the graph Time complexity: O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges

      • setEdgeWeight

        public void setEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge,
                          double weight)
        Sets the weight of the specified edge to the given double value.
        Specified by:
        setEdgeWeight in interface org.jgrapht.Graph<V,​W>
        Parameters:
        edge - the edge whose weight is to be set
        weight - the new weight value
        Throws:
        java.lang.UnsupportedOperationException - if the weight type is not supported Time complexity: O(1)

      • setEdgeWeight

        public void setEdgeWeight​(DEdge<V,​W> edge,
                          W weight)
        Sets edge weight
        Parameters:
        edge - to set weight to
        weight - to be assigned Time complexity: O(1)

      • equals

        public boolean equals​(java.lang.Object obj)
        Compares this graph to the specified object for equality.
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        obj - the object to compare with
        Returns:
        true if the graphs are equal, false otherwise Time complexity: O(V + E), where V is the number of vertices and E is the number of edges
